entries.text_ar: God will bring to naught the deeds of those who disbelieve and bar others from the way of God.He said:He brought them to naught because of their utterance of words that had no reality (áļĨaqÄŦqa) behind them.His words:
That is because God is patron (mawlÄ) of those who believeïŋ―That is, by His good pleasure, love and His keeping them in the station of proximity (qurb).His words:

And how many a town, mightier in power than your town, which expelled you, have We destroyed and they had none to help them.In this verse there is proof of his [MuáļĨammadïŋ―s] superiority over KalÄŦm [Moses], because he did not leave out of fear of them in the way that Moses did, but he left in the way that God, Exalted is He, said in the words: which expelled you. He did not say ïŋ―you leftïŋ―, nor âļĒïŋ―you fledïŋ―âļĢ, nor ïŋ―you felt afraidïŋ―, for he [acted] by God and for God at all times, and it never happened that his attention was diverted to other [than Him] in any particular situation.His words:

Is he who follows a clear sign from his Lordïŋ―?He said:The believer has a clear explanation (bayÄn) from his Lord and whoever has a clear proof (bayyina) from his Lord adheres to following the established ways of the Prophet (sunan).His words, Exalted is He:Know, then, that there is no god but Godïŋ―He said:All people are dead except those who know (ĘŋulamÄĘū), and for this reason He summoned His Prophet to the abode of life (maáļĨall al-áļĨayÄt), through knowledge (Ęŋilm), with His words: Knowïŋ―His words:
ïŋ―and forgiveness from their Lord. He said:Forgiveness from their Lord in Paradise is the lights of God which cover them at their vision of Him. His words:

ïŋ―And ask forgiveness for your sin and for the believing men and womenïŋ―He said:That is, ïŋ―Seek forgiveness for the desire (himma) of the natural self (nafs al-áđ­abĘŋ).ïŋ― The Prophet said, ïŋ―There is not one among us who has not had the desire [to do something] and then transgressed.ïŋ―This means that a [personïŋ―s] lower self (nafs) intended something, overwhelming the heart (ĘŋalÄïŋ―l-qalb), for the immediate [gratification of its share of] lusts (ĘŋÄjil al-shahawÄt), but then thatperson turned away from that [intent] and sought forgiveness from God, just as the Prophet said: ïŋ―Truly, my heart becomes clouded, and truly I ask forgiveness from God, Exalted is He, seventy times every day.ïŋ― His words:

ïŋ―or is it that they have locks on their hearts?He said:God, Exalted is He, created the hearts, and secured them with locks. Then He made the realitiesof faith (áļĨaqÄĘūiq al-ÄŦmÄn) the keys to hearts. With those keys, the only hearts He opened to realisation (taáļĨqÄŦq) were those of His friends (awliyÄĘū), messengers (rusul) Ã, and the veracious (áđĢiddÄŦqÅŦn). The rest of people leave this world without the locks on their hearts being opened. The renunciants (zuhhÄd), devout worshippers (ĘŋubbÄd), and scholars (ĘŋulamÄĘū) will leave this world with locked hearts because they sought the keys to them with the intellect (Ęŋaql), and thus strayed from the path. If only they had sought them by having recourse to God-given success (tawfÄŦq) and grace (faáļl), they would have attained them [the keys]. The key is to know that God is taking care of you (qÄĘūim Ęŋalayka), and watching over (raqÄŦb) your bodily members, and to know that works are not complete without sincerity (ikhlÄáđĢ) accompanied by heedfulness (murÄqÄba) [of God].His words:

ïŋ―Obey God and obey the Messengerïŋ―That is, in revering (taĘŋášÄŦm) God. ïŋ―Do not render your own works void, that is, by seeing them as coming from yourselves and by seeking recompense (aĘŋwÄáļ) from your Lord, for sincere works are those that are done without seeking recompense.His words, Exalted is He:

ïŋ―God is the Self-Sufficient One, while you are the needyïŋ―.Gnosis (maĘŋrifa) of the secret [divine] mystery (sirr) is [attained] entirely through neediness (faqr), and that is Godïŋ―s secret (sirr AllÄh). The knowledge of [oneïŋ―s] neediness for God, Exalted is He, is the corrective to the knowledge that [one has] richness (ghinÄ) through God, Mighty and Majestic is He. But God, Glorified and Exalted is He, knows best.His words, Exalted is He:

He will guide them and dispose their minds aright.He said:This means that He will guide them in their graves to answer Munkar and NakÄŦr correctly; and dispose their minds aright, that is, He will bring about the commencement of the reward in their hearts [in the grave] (jazÄĘū), and in the Hereafter, the delight of the meeting [with Him] (ladhdhat al-liqÄĘū) at the moment of the manifestation (tajallÄŦ) of unveiling (mukÄshafa), as a face-to-face encounter (kifÄáļĨan), and thereupon, [His] taking care (tawallÄŦ) of them, as when He says:
